implemented: Two sibling fixes in `tldr vuln`. (1) Removed the `MAX_DIRECTORY_FILES = 1000` const and its guard in `collect_files` (vuln.rs:499). After VAL-001 the walker honors .gitignore + default excludes structurally, so the legacy numeric cap served no safety purpose and only silently truncated input on medium repos (dub's 3928 .ts/.tsx files were capped at 1000). The per-file `MAX_FILE_SIZE = 10 MB` parser safety cap is kept (unrelated concern). (2) Added language autodetection in `VulnArgs::run()` when `--lang` is omitted: resolves `effective_lang` via `Language::from_directory` for directory paths or `Language::from_path` for single-file paths. If the detected language is NOT in the command's native-analysis set {Python, Rust}, returns a new `RemainingError::AutodetectUnsupported` variant with exit code 2 and a stderr message naming the detected language and pointing the user at `--lang python` / `--lang rust`. If detection returns `None` (empty tree), preserves the historical empty-report-exit-0 behavior. Explicit `--lang <L>` always bypasses the detect-and-error path (VAL-001 contract preserved — users who pass `--lang typescript` get the multi-language pattern scanner via `tldr_core::security::vuln`). Added helper `is_natively_analyzed(Language) -> bool` in vuln.rs with documentation of why the supported set is {Python, Rust} (those have dedicated taint / unsafe-pattern analyzers; other languages fall through to `scan_vulnerabilities` which is meaningful but weaker line-pattern matching). Files changed: `crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs` (+61 -12 LOC), `crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/error.rs` (+27 -5 LOC), `crates/tldr-cli/tests/vuln_autodetect_tests.rs` (+290 LOC, new file).

added.name: test_vuln_autodetects_rust
added.verifies: VAL-006: Cargo.toml present + .rs + decoy .py. Autodetect picks Rust; scan sees exactly 2 .rs files (decoy excluded). Proves autodetect overrides the pre-VAL-006 None-fallback that would scan both extensions.

added.name: test_vuln_autodetects_python
added.verifies: VAL-006: pyproject.toml + .py + decoy .rs. Autodetect picks Python; scan sees exactly 1 .py file (decoy excluded).

added.name: test_vuln_errors_on_unsupported_autodetected_lang
added.verifies: VAL-006: tsconfig.json + .ts. Exit 2. Stderr contains 'not yet supported' or 'typescript' AND mentions '--lang python' or '--lang rust'.

added.name: test_vuln_honors_explicit_lang_typescript
added.verifies: VAL-006 / VAL-001 regression: `--lang typescript` bypasses autodetect-and-error, scan runs through scan_vulnerabilities path. Exit 0, valid report with files_scanned field.

added.name: test_vuln_no_detectable_lang_empty_dir
added.verifies: VAL-006: empty tempdir. Autodetect returns None. Empty report, exit 0 (historical behavior preserved — no error on no-input).

added.name: test_vuln_no_cap_on_large_repos
added.verifies: VAL-006: 1500 .rs files in a flat dir with explicit --lang rust. files_scanned >= 1500. Proves the MAX_DIRECTORY_FILES=1000 cap is removed.

added.name: test_exit_codes (extended)
added.verifies: VAL-006: new variant RemainingError::AutodetectUnsupported has exit code 2 (distinct from exit 1 general failure).

checks.command: tldr vuln . --format json --quiet (in tldr-code repo, no --lang)

discoveries.finding: The task prompt assumed `only Python and Rust have real analysis paths` in the taint engine. Verified in crates/tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs:140-286 (get_sources) and 292-617 (get_sinks) that scan_vulnerabilities supports line-pattern source/sink detection for 17 languages: Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Go, Rust, Java, C, C++, Ruby, Kotlin, Swift, CSharp, Scala, PHP, Lua, Luau, Elixir, OCaml. However, the dedicated tree-sitter intra-procedural taint tracker (analyze_python_file + analyze_function) is Python-only, and the line-scanner for unsafe-code patterns (analyze_rust_file) is Rust-only. Other languages fall through to scan_vulnerabilities which is meaningful but weaker than the dedicated paths. The VAL-006 autodetect-supported set is therefore drawn at {Python, Rust} — the languages with dedicated backends. Explicit --lang <L> still routes through scan_vulnerabilities for other languages, preserving the VAL-001 user-choice contract.

RemainingError::UnsupportedLanguage (exit code 1) semantically differs from the new RemainingError::AutodetectUnsupported (exit code 2). The former fires when the user explicitly passes --lang <L> and L cannot be handled by the command (error attempting to start). The latter fires when the command autodetected L and chose not to run (scan not attempted). Split into two variants rather than overloading UnsupportedLanguage so CI/tooling can tell apart 'ran and failed' from 'didn't run'.

The per-file MAX_FILE_SIZE = 10 MB cap is genuinely necessary — it prevents tree-sitter or the line-scanner from locking on a pathological large file. MAX_DIRECTORY_FILES = 1000 predated VAL-001's walker hardening; with .gitignore + default excludes honored structurally, no numeric file-count cap is needed. Removing MAX_DIRECTORY_FILES while keeping MAX_FILE_SIZE is the right decomposition.

Pre-implementation, 2 of the 6 VAL-006 tests passed trivially: test_vuln_no_detectable_lang_empty_dir (walker naturally finds zero files in an empty tempdir) and test_vuln_honors_explicit_lang_typescript (M1 already wired --lang correctly through is_supported_source_file). The strengthened autodetect tests (with decoy files of the opposite language) are what specifically exercise VAL-006's autodetect wiring — the original formulation (single Rust file, no decoy) would have passed pre-fix because the None-fallback of {py, rs} happens to scan .rs files. Test strengthening was load-bearing for TDD red-to-green discipline.

issues.issue: Pre-existing cli-lib test failure: commands::bugbot::text_format::tests::test_text_format_generic_evidence_shows_numbers. Identified in M4 report as unrelated to VAL-004. I did not touch crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/bugbot/text_format.rs; failure persists from prior working-tree state. Not caused by VAL-006.

Pre-existing workspace fmt drift in crates/tldr-core/src/analysis/change_impact.rs (multi-line panic! macro re-wrapping). Fails cargo fmt --check at the workspace level. I did not touch that file; drift is from prior working-tree state. My three edited files pass rustfmt --check individually.

conventions.convention: Autodetect-and-error pattern: when a CLI arg (--lang, --target, etc.) can be autodetected from project context but the detected value lies outside the command's supported set, emit a distinct exit code (2) and a stderr message that (a) names the detected value, (b) points the user at an explicit override. Preserves 'scan not attempted' vs 'scan failed' distinction in CI pipelines.

Structural bounds over numeric caps: after VAL-001 consolidated walking under ProjectWalker, numeric file-count caps (MAX_DIRECTORY_FILES) are vestigial — the walker already honors .gitignore + default vendor excludes. Per-file caps (MAX_FILE_SIZE) remain appropriate because they protect a different concern (parser-level stalls on pathological single files).

Native-analysis set as a function of backend, not extension list. `is_natively_analyzed(Language) -> bool` encodes which languages have a dedicated taint/pattern backend in vuln.rs vs fall through to scan_vulnerabilities. When adding a new dedicated backend (e.g., a Go-specific taint tracker), update the matcher in is_natively_analyzed; autodetect automatically picks up the new language without additional plumbing.
